## Explanation

This implements the **Knuth-Morris-Pratt (KMP) string search algorithm**, which finds the first occurrence of a `pattern` within a `text`, returning its starting index (or `-1` if not found).

It works in two phases: first, it builds a **Longest Proper Prefix-Suffix (LPS) table** from the pattern, which encodes how far to "fall back" on a mismatch to avoid redundant comparisons; then it uses that table to scan the text in a single left-to-right pass, achieving **O(n + m)** time complexity instead of the naive O(n·m).
